verses.1: Now King Solomon was king over all Israel.
verses.2: These were his officials: Azariah the son of Zadok was the priest;
verses.3: Elihoreph and Ahijah, the sons of Shisha were scribes; Jehoshaphat the son of Ahilud was the secretary;
verses.4: and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was over the army; and Zadok and Abiathar were priests;
verses.5: and Azariah the son of Nathan was over the deputies; and Zabud the son of Nathan, a priest, was the king’s confidant;
verses.6: and Ahishar was over the household; and Adoniram the son of Abda was over the forced labor.
verses.7: Solomon had twelve deputies over all Israel, who provided food for the king and his household; each deputy had to provide food for a month in the year.
verses.8: And these were their names: Ben-hur, in the hill country of Ephraim;
verses.9: Ben-deker in Makaz and Shaalbim, and Beth-shemesh, and Elonbeth-hanan;
verses.10: Ben-hesed in Arubboth (Socoh was his and all the land of Hepher);
verses.11: Ben-abinadab in all the hills of Dor (Taphath the daughter of Solomon was his wife);
verses.12: Baana the son of Ahilud in Taanach and Megiddo, and all Beth-shean which is beside Zarethan below Jezreel, from Beth-shean to Abel-meholah as far as the other side of Jokmeam;
verses.13: Ben-geber in Ramoth-gilead (the villages of Jair, the son of Manasseh, which are in Gilead were his: the region of Argob, which is in Bashan, sixty great cities with walls and bronze bars were his);
verses.14: Ahinadab the son of Iddo in Mahanaim;
verses.15: Ahimaaz in Naphtali (he also married Basemath the daughter of Solomon);
verses.16: Baana the son of Hushai in Asher and Bealoth;
verses.17: Jehoshaphat the son of Paruah in Issachar;
verses.18: Shimei the son of Ela in Benjamin;
verses.19: Geber the son of Uri in the land of Gilead, the country of Sihon king of the Amorites and of Og king of Bashan; and he was the only deputy who was in the land.
verses.20: Judah and Israel were as numerous as the sand that is on the seashore in abundance; they were eating, drinking, and rejoicing.
verses.21: Now Solomon was ruling over all the kingdoms from the Euphrates River to the land of the Philistines and to the border of Egypt; they brought tribute and served Solomon all the days of his life.
verses.22: Solomon’s provision for one day was thirty kors of fine flour and sixty kors of meal,
verses.23: ten fat oxen, twenty pasture-fed oxen, and a hundred sheep, besides deer, gazelles, roebucks, and fattened geese.
verses.24: For he was ruling over everything west of the Euphrates River, from Tiphsah even to Gaza, over all the kings west of the River; and he had peace on all sides surrounding him.
verses.25: So Judah and Israel lived securely, everyone under his vine and his fig tree, from Dan even to Beersheba, all the days of Solomon.
verses.26: Solomon had forty thousand stalls of horses for his chariots, and twelve thousand horsemen.
verses.27: And those deputies provided food for King Solomon and all who came to King Solomon’s table, each in his month; they allowed nothing to be lacking.
verses.28: They also brought barley and straw for the war horses and baggage horses to the place where it was required, each deputy according to his duty.
verses.29: Now God gave Solomon wisdom and very great discernment and breadth of mind, like the sand that is on the seashore.
verses.30: Solomon’s wisdom surpassed the wisdom of all the people of the east and all the wisdom of Egypt.
verses.31: For he was wiser than all other people, more than Ethan the Ezrahite, Heman, Calcol, and Darda, the sons of Mahol; and his fame was known in all the surrounding nations.
verses.32: He also told three thousand proverbs, and his songs numbered 1,005.
verses.33: He told of trees, from the cedar that is in Lebanon even to the hyssop that grows on the wall; he told also of animals, birds, crawling things, and fish.
verses.34: People came from all the nations to hear the wisdom of Solomon, from all the kings of the earth who had heard of his wisdom.
